title: Clear Directions
learningGoal: Master the pronunciation of key directional words (straight, right, left) and the intonation of asking for directions.
instructions: 方向を表す単語（straight, right, left）の正しい発音と、道を尋ねる時のイントネーション（How do I get to...?）を練習します。マイクに向かってリピートしてください。(Practice pronouncing directional words and the intonation for asking directions. Repeat into the microphone.)

targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: Make sure you pronounce the 'r' clearly, and the 'gh' is silent. Stray-t.
targetPhrasesOrWords.commonMispronunciation: Saying 'sutoreito' with too many vowel sounds.

targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: Start with a strong 'r' sound (not an 'L'). The 'gh' is silent. R-eye-t.
Start with a clear 'L' sound (tongue behind top teeth). L-ef-t.

targetPhrasesOrWords.commonMispronunciation: Saying 'akurosu' with a long 'u' sound at the end.

targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: The 't' in next and the 't' in to blend together into one sound. Nex-too.

targetPhrasesOrWords.transliteration: エクスキューズ ミー
targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: Make sure the 's' in excuse sounds like a 'z'. Ex-cyooz me.

targetPhrasesOrWords.transliteration: ハウ ドゥー アイ ゲット トゥ
targetPhrasesOrWords.baseTranslation: 〜にはどうやって行けばいいですか
targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: Link 'How do I' together smoothly. How-doo-eye get-too.
targetPhrasesOrWords.commonMispronunciation: Saying each word separately like a robot.

targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: The 't' in the middle often sounds like a soft 'd' in American English. Hos-pi-dul.
The stress is on the 'ze'. Myoo-ZEE-um.
targetPhrasesOrWords.commonMispronunciation: Saying 'myuujiamu' with the stress on the first syllable.

expectedPronunciationText: straight right left across next to Excuse me How do I get to station hospital museum

feedbackRules.onPass: Excellent! Your pronunciation is very clear. People will easily understand your directions.
feedbackRules.onFail: Please try again. Focus on the difference between the 'R' in 'right' and the 'L' in 'left'.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good effort! Remember to blend 'next' and 'to' together: 'nex-too'.

adminCorrectAnswerSet.exactCorrectTranscript: straight right left across next to Excuse me How do I get to station hospital museum
acceptedVariants: straight right left across next to excuse me how do i get to station hospital museum
adminCorrectAnswerSet.nativeScriptReference: straight right left across next to Excuse me How do I get to station hospital museum
adminCorrectAnswerSet.transliteration: strayt rite left uhcross nextoo excyooz me howdooeye getoo stayshun hospidul myoozeeum

audioAssets.audioTranscript: straight, right, left, across, next to, Excuse me, How do I get to, station, hospital, museum

hintText: Make sure you roll your tongue back for 'right' and touch your teeth for 'left'.
commonMistakes: Mixing up the L and R sounds in left and right.
